heading: Coltsville National Historical Park

children.heading: City
children.label: 1 City
children.text: The term “city” means the city of Hartford, Connecticut.

children.heading: Commission
children.label: 2 Commission
children.text: The term “Commission” means the Coltsville National Historical Park Advisory Commission established by subsection (k)(1).

children.heading: Historic District
children.label: 3 Historic District
children.text: The term “Historic District” means the Coltsville Historic District.

children.heading: Map
children.label: 4 Map
children.text: The term “map” means the map entitled “Coltsville National Historical Park—Proposed Boundary”, numbered T25/102087, and dated .May 11, 2010

children.heading: Park
children.label: 5 Park
children.text: The term “park” means the Coltsville National Historical Park in the State of Connecticut.

children.heading: Secretary
children.label: 6 Secretary
children.text: The term “Secretary” means the Secretary of the Interior.

children.heading: State
children.label: 7 State
children.text: The term “State” means the State of Connecticut.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Subject to paragraph (2), there is established in the State a unit of the National Park System to be known as the “Coltsville National Historical Park”.

children.label: A
children.text: the Secretary has acquired by donation sufficient land or an interest in land within the boundary of the park to constitute a manageable unit;

children.label: B
children.text: the State, city, or private property owner, as appropriate, has entered into a written agreement with the Secretary to donate at least 10,000 square feet of space in the Colt Armory Complex which would include facilities for park administration and visitor services; and

children.label: C
children.text: the Secretary has entered into a written agreement with the State, city, or other public entity, as appropriate, providing that land owned by the State, city, or other public entity within the Coltsville Historic District shall be managed consistent with this section.

children.heading: Notice
children.label: 3 Notice
children.text: Not later than 30 days after the date on which the Secretary makes a determination under paragraph (2), the Secretary shall publish in the Federal Register notice of the establishment of the park.
The East Armory.
The Church of the Good Shepherd.
The Caldwell/Colt Memorial Parish House.
Colt Park.
The Potsdam Cottages.
Armsmear.
The James Colt House.

children.heading: Availability of map
children.label: d Availability of map
children.text: The map shall be on file and available for public inspection in appropriate offices of the National Park Service.

children.heading: Collections
children.label: e Collections
children.text: The Secretary may enter into a written agreement with the State of Connecticut State Library, Wadsworth Atheneum, and the Colt Trust, or other public entities, as appropriate, to gain appropriate access to Colt-related artifacts for the purposes of having items routinely on display in the East Armory or within other areas of the park to enhance the visitor experience.

children.label: A
children.text: this section; and

children.label: i
children.text: the National Park Service Organic Act ( et seq.); and16 U.S.C. 11See References in Text note below.1

children.label: ii
children.text: the Act of ( et seq.).August 21, 193516 U.S.C. 4611

children.label: A
children.text: to exercise civil and criminal jurisdiction; or

children.label: B
children.text: to carry out State laws (including regulations) and rules on non-Federal land located within the boundary of the park.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: As the Secretary determines to be appropriate to carry out this section, the Secretary may enter into cooperative agreements to carry out this section, under which the Secretary may identify, interpret, restore, rehabilitate, and provide technical assistance for the preservation of nationally significant properties within the boundary of the park.

children.label: A
children.text: conducting visitors through the properties; and

children.label: B
children.text: interpreting the properties for the public.

children.heading: Changes or alterations
children.label: 3 Changes or alterations
children.text: No changes or alterations shall be made to any properties covered by a cooperative agreement entered into under paragraph (1) unless the Secretary and the other party to the agreement agree to the changes or alterations.

children.label: A
children.text: the amounts made available to the project by the United States; or

children.label: B
children.text: the portion of the increased value of the project attributable to the amounts made available under this subsection, as determined at the time of the conversion, use, or disposal.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: As a condition of the receipt of funds under this subsection, the Secretary shall require that any Federal funds made available under a cooperative agreement shall be matched on a 1-to-1 basis by non-Federal funds.

children.heading: Form
children.label: B Form
children.text: With the approval of the Secretary, the non-Federal share required under subparagraph (A) may be in the form of donated property, goods, or services from a non-Federal source, fairly valued.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The Secretary is authorized to acquire land and interests in land by donation, purchase with donated or appropriated funds, or exchange, except that land or interests in land owned by the State or any political subdivision of the State may be acquired only by donation.

children.heading: No condemnation
children.label: 2 No condemnation
children.text: The Secretary may not acquire any land or interest in land for the purposes of this section by condemnation.

children.heading: Technical assistance and public interpretation
children.label: i Technical assistance and public interpretation
children.text: The Secretary may provide technical assistance and public interpretation of related historic and cultural resources within the boundary of the historic district.

children.label: A
children.text: ; andsection 100502 of title 54

children.label: B
children.text: other applicable laws.

children.heading: Cost share
children.label: 2 Cost share
children.text: The management plan shall include provisions that identify costs to be shared by the Federal Government, the State, and the city, and other public or private entities or individuals for necessary capital improvements to, and maintenance and operations of, the park.

children.label: A
children.text: the Committee on Natural Resources of the House of Representatives; and

children.label: B
children.text: the Committee on Energy and Natural Resources of the Senate.

children.heading: Coltsville National Historical Park Advisory Commission
children.label: k Coltsville National Historical Park Advisory Commission

children.heading: Establishment
children.label: 1 Establishment
children.text: There is established a Commission to be known as the “Coltsville National Historical Park Advisory Commission”.

children.heading: Duty
children.label: 2 Duty
children.text: The Commission shall advise the Secretary in the development and implementation of the management plan.

children.label: i
children.text: 2 members shall be appointed after consideration of recommendations submitted by the Governor of the State;

children.label: ii
children.text: 1 member shall be appointed after consideration of recommendations submitted by the State Senate President;

children.label: iii
children.text: 1 member shall be appointed after consideration of recommendations submitted by the Speaker of the State House of Representatives;

children.label: iv
children.text: 2 members shall be appointed after consideration of recommendations submitted by the Mayor of Hartford, Connecticut;

children.label: v
children.text: 2 members shall be appointed after consideration of recommendations submitted by Connecticut’s 2 United States Senators;

children.label: vi
children.text: 1 member shall be appointed after consideration of recommendations submitted by Connecticut’s First Congressional District Representative;

children.label: vii
children.text: 2 members shall have experience with national parks and historic preservation;

children.label: viii
children.text: all appointments must have significant experience with and knowledge of the Coltsville Historic District; and

children.label: ix
children.text: 1 member of the Commission must live in the Sheldon/Charter Oak neighborhood within the Coltsville Historic District.

children.label: i
children.text: the date that is 30 days after the date on which the Secretary has received all of the recommendations for appointments under subparagraph (A); or

children.label: ii
children.text: the date that is 30 days after the park is established.

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: A member shall be appointed for a term of 3 years.

children.heading: Reappointment
children.label: ii Reappointment
children.text: A member may be reappointed for not more than 1 additional term.

children.heading: Vacancies
children.label: B Vacancies
children.text: A vacancy on the Commission shall be filled in the same manner as the original appointment was made.

children.label: A
children.text: the Chairperson; or

children.label: B
children.text: a majority of the members of the Commission.

children.heading: Quorum
children.label: 6 Quorum
children.text: A majority of the Commission shall constitute a quorum.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The Commission shall select a Chairperson and Vice Chairperson from among the members of the Commission.

children.heading: Vice Chairperson
children.label: B Vice Chairperson
children.text: The Vice Chairperson shall serve as Chairperson in the absence of the Chairperson.

children.heading: Term
children.label: C Term
children.text: A member may serve as Chairperson or Vice Chairperson for not more than 1 year in each office.

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: Members of the Commission shall serve without compensation.

children.heading: Travel expenses
children.label: ii Travel expenses
children.text: Members of the Commission shall be allowed travel expenses, including per diem in lieu of subsistence, at rates authorized for an employee of an agency under subchapter I of chapter 57 of title 5, while away from the home or regular place of business of the member in the performance of the duty of the Commission.

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: The Secretary shall provide the Commission with any staff members and technical assistance that the Secretary, after consultation with the Commission, determines to be appropriate to enable the Commission to carry out the duty of the Commission.

children.heading: Detail of employees
children.label: ii Detail of employees
children.text: The Secretary may accept the services of personnel detailed from the State or any political subdivision of the State.

children.heading: Nonapplicability of chapter 10 of title 5
children.label: 9 Nonapplicability of chapter 10 of title 5
children.text: shall not apply to the Commission.Section 1013(b) of title 5

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Unless extended under subparagraph (B), the Commission shall terminate on the date that is 10 years after .December 19, 2014

children.heading: Recommendation
children.label: i Recommendation
children.text: Eight years after , the Commission shall make a recommendation to the Secretary if a body of its nature is still necessary to advise on the development of the park.December 19, 2014

children.heading: Term of extension
children.label: ii Term of extension
children.text: If, based on a recommendation under clause (i), the Secretary determines that the Commission is still necessary, the Secretary may extend the life of the Commission for not more than 10 years.
